data.title: Vitalik advocates raising Ethereum’s gas limit because it helps resist censorship and improve L2 interoperability and security during disasters
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Vitalik Buterin advocates raising Ethereum's gas limit on the grounds that it is conducive to censorship resistance, L2 interoperability, and security in the event of a disaster.
Earlier, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin published the latest article "Why the L1 Gas Limit Still Needs to Be Raised in L2-dominated Ethereum". The article stated that an important recent discussion in the Ethereum roadmap is about how to increase the L1 gas limit. Recently, the L1 gas limit increased from 30 million to 36 million, a 20% increase in capacity. Many people support further substantial increases in the near future. These increases are safe because of recent and upcoming technical improvements: improved efficiency of Ethereum clients, reduced need to store old history due to EIP-4444 (see roadmap), and future stateless clients. However, before we go down this path, it is important to ask a question: In the context of a Rollup-centric roadmap, is raising the L1 gas limit the right thing to do in the long run? We don't want to end up facing the centralization risks brought about by high L1 usage without really being sure that we will benefit from it.

data.title: Digital asset financial services provider Hex Trust announces acquisition of Byte Trading
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Digital asset financial services provider Hex Trust recently announced the acquisition of Byte Trading. The specific acquisition amount has not been announced. The move is aimed at enhancing Hex's market and main services for institutional investors, and also marks the company's transformation from a custodian to a full-service financial institution.
By integrating Byte Trading's low-latency trading platform into its compliance infrastructure, Hex Trust is committed to providing seamless execution and expanded institutional-grade services.
Earlier news, Hex Trust announced the completion of a new round of strategic financing, led by Morgan Creek Digital and participated by Injective. Although the company did not disclose the specific amount of this round of financing, it disclosed that its total financing has now exceeded US$100 million. Hex Trust also stated that it will launch a C round of financing later this year and make potential strategic acquisitions. Its business currently covers Hong Kong, Singapore, Dubai, France and Italy.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Advocates Increasing Ethereum's Gas Limit
data.description:
According to Odaily, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has proposed raising the gas limit on Ethereum's Layer 1 (L1) network. He argues that this move could help resist censorship, enhance Layer 2 (L2) interoperability, and improve security during catastrophic events. Buterin recently published an article titled "Why We Still Need to Raise the L1 Gas Limit in an L2-Dominated Ethereum," discussing the ongoing debate about increasing the L1 gas limit.

The gas limit was recently increased from 30 million to 36 million, marking a 20% capacity boost. Many in the community support further significant increases in the near future. These increases are considered safe due to recent and upcoming technological improvements, such as enhanced efficiency of Ethereum clients, reduced need for storing old history due to EIP-4444, and the development of future stateless clients.

However, Buterin emphasizes the importance of considering whether raising the L1 gas limit is the right long-term strategy within a rollup-centric roadmap. He cautions against the potential centralization risks associated with high L1 usage if the benefits are not clearly established.

data.title: Binance exec reiterates rogue Nigerian lawmaker demanded $150 million bribe in exchange for dropping lawsuit against Binance
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Binance Financial Crime Compliance Director Tigran Gambaryan recently reiterated that rogue Nigerian lawmakers had demanded a $150 million bribe in exchange for dropping a lawsuit against Binance. The lawmakers wanted to transfer the funds directly to their cryptocurrency wallets, and they used "fake cameras and media" to make meetings with visiting Binance executives look legitimate.
In May 2024, it was reported that Binance CEO Richard Teng accused members of the Nigerian House of Representatives Financial Crimes Committee (HCFC) of demanding a $150 million bribe. At the time, Nigerian Information Minister Mohammed Idris denied the allegations, calling it a diversion strategy by the company "eager to cover up the serious criminal charges it faces."
However, Gambaryan insisted that the attempt was indeed made with the participation of the Nigerian Department of State Services (DSS). It also stated: "The Nigerian government has been publicly claiming that $26 billion of mysterious funds have flowed out of Nigeria (through Binance), which is complete nonsense. This information was provided at their request and is just the cumulative transaction data of Nigerians on the platform. The money has not flowed out of Nigeria - it's just that people are buying and selling cryptocurrencies. For example, if you trade 100 times with $100, that's $10,000 in trading volume, but in fact, you only used $100. Again, this is just another example of them lying to cover up a fake investigation." Just after Gambaryan shared these explosive details, Nigerian Information Minister Idris issued a statement denying it. However, the statement acknowledged the bribery allegations, but Idris pointed out that it was the Nigerian government that launched the investigation, "although no one has formally filed a lawsuit." Its statement also revealed that the Nigerian government rejected the US's offer to pay $5 million in exchange for Gambaryan's release. (Bitcoin.com)

data.title: Former CFTC Chairman: Bitcoin’s design is destined to be scarce
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Former US CFTC Chairman Chris Giancarlo said: "Bitcoin's design is destined to be scarce. The number of Bitcoins you own can never exceed a certain amount." (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Former CFTC Chair Highlights Bitcoin's Inherent Scarcity
data.description: According to Odaily, former U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Chairman Chris Giancarlo emphasized the inherent scarcity of Bitcoin. He stated that the design of Bitcoin ensures its limited supply, making it impossible for the number of Bitcoins one holds to exceed a certain amount.

data.title: Foreign media: "Tesla is worried that Musk's political involvement will harm the company, and some executives suggest that he should resign"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: An internal recording shows that at a recent employee meeting held by a department of Tesla, Tesla employees and senior executives publicly expressed their concerns about Musk, believing that he is undermining the company and its sustainable development mission. Two Tesla employees said that there is growing dissatisfaction with Musk throughout the company. Some employees believe that Musk's service to the Trump administration has reduced his attention to Tesla. In addition, because Trump is a controversial figure, there is growing concern within the company that Musk has established a close relationship with him, believing that this has damaged the company's brand image among some consumers. One employee said that some senior executives hinted in employee meetings that the company might be better off if Musk resigned. (Washington Post)

data.title: FBI Helps 4,300 Potential Victims of Cryptocurrency Scams Avoid $285 Million in Losses
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) recently announced that "Operation Level Up" has successfully prevented victims from losing millions of dollars due to cryptocurrency investment fraud. The agency explained that the program identifies individuals who are being defrauded and intervenes before they suffer losses. It pointed out: "As of January 2025, the FBI has notified more than 4,300 potential victims, saving them approximately $285 million in losses." "Operation Level Up" was launched in 2024 and targets increasingly sophisticated cryptocurrency scams, including scammers using fake investment platforms and social engineering to deceive victims. (Bitcoin.com)

data.title: FBI's Operation Level Up Prevents Cryptocurrency Fraud Losses
data.description: According to Odaily, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) recently announced the success of 'Operation Level Up,' a campaign aimed at preventing millions of dollars in losses from cryptocurrency investment fraud. The initiative identifies individuals at risk of being scammed and intervenes before they incur financial losses. As of January 2025, the FBI has alerted over 4,300 potential victims, helping them avoid approximately $285 million in losses. Launched in 2024, 'Operation Level Up' targets increasingly sophisticated cryptocurrency scams, including those involving fake investment platforms and social engineering tactics.

data.title: Two traders who lost about 50% on LIBRA bought a total of $926,000 of LIBRA again 4 hours ago
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Spot On Chain monitoring, despite the LIBRA Rug Pull controversy, some traders are still buying in large quantities after suffering losses: 
1. Trader "6jnGR" spent 2,536 SOL (478,000 US dollars) to buy 1.19 million LIBRA about 4 hours ago, and previously lost 4,570 SOL (885,000 US dollars, -48%). 
2. Trader "AjqxR" spent 448,000 US dollars USDC to buy 980,000 LIBRA about 4 hours ago, and previously lost 937,000 US dollars (-52%).
